verses.text: God bids Moses hew two new tables and come up the mount alone. New tables. Grace renews the broken covenant.
God bids Moses hew two new tables and come up the mount alone. New tables. Grace renews the broken covenant.
God bids Moses hew two new tables and come up the mount alone. New tables. Grace renews the broken covenant.
God bids Moses hew two new tables and come up the mount alone. New tables. Grace renews the broken covenant.
The LORD proclaims His name: 'merciful and gracious, longsuffering... forgiving iniquity,' yet not clearing the guilty. God's name. Mercy and justice meet in Him.
The LORD proclaims His name: 'merciful and gracious, longsuffering... forgiving iniquity,' yet not clearing the guilty. God's name. Mercy and justice meet in Him.
The LORD proclaims His name: 'merciful and gracious, longsuffering... forgiving iniquity,' yet not clearing the guilty. God's name. Mercy and justice meet in Him.
Moses worships and pleads God go among them and pardon their sin. Worship. Seeing God bows the heart.
Moses worships and pleads God go among them and pardon their sin. Worship. Seeing God bows the heart.
God makes a covenant to do marvels and drive out the nations. Covenant. He renews His promise.
God makes a covenant to do marvels and drive out the nations. Covenant. He renews His promise.
Make no league with the land's peoples; 'the LORD, whose name is Jealous.' No league. Compromise leads to idolatry.
Make no league with the land's peoples; 'the LORD, whose name is Jealous.' No league. Compromise leads to idolatry.
Make no league with the land's peoples; 'the LORD, whose name is Jealous.' No league. Compromise leads to idolatry.
Break down their altars; make no molten gods. Purge idols. Tolerated idols ensnare.
Break down their altars; make no molten gods. Purge idols. Tolerated idols ensnare.
Break down their altars; make no molten gods. Purge idols. Tolerated idols ensnare.
Keep the feast of unleavened bread; every firstborn is the LORD's. Feasts. Worship marks the redeemed.
Keep the feast of unleavened bread; every firstborn is the LORD's. Feasts. Worship marks the redeemed.
Keep the feast of unleavened bread; every firstborn is the LORD's. Feasts. Worship marks the redeemed.
Six days work, rest the seventh; keep the three yearly feasts. Sabbath. God orders their times.
Six days work, rest the seventh; keep the three yearly feasts. Sabbath. God orders their times.
Six days work, rest the seventh; keep the three yearly feasts. Sabbath. God orders their times.
Six days work, rest the seventh; keep the three yearly feasts. Sabbath. God orders their times.
Bring the firstfruits; 'thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk.' Firstfruits. Give God the best.
Bring the firstfruits; 'thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk.' Firstfruits. Give God the best.
Moses is forty days with God, neither eating nor drinking, and writes the words. With God. He is sustained in God's presence.
Moses is forty days with God, neither eating nor drinking, and writes the words. With God. He is sustained in God's presence.
Coming down, his face shines, and the people fear to come near. Shining face. Communion with God leaves a glow.
Coming down, his face shines, and the people fear to come near. Shining face. Communion with God leaves a glow.
Coming down, his face shines, and the people fear to come near. Shining face. Communion with God leaves a glow.
He veils his face after speaking God's words to them. The veil. He covers the fading glory.
He veils his face after speaking God's words to them. The veil. He covers the fading glory.
He veils his face after speaking God's words to them. The veil. He covers the fading glory.
He veils his face after speaking God's words to them. The veil. He covers the fading glory.
